paper_title: Long cycles in vertex transitive digraphs
summary: Question 4.3 of arXiv:2602.16333 asks whether vertex transitivity is sufficient to guarantee that any two longest directed cycles in a connected vertex-transitive digraph share a common vertex. The closely related paper arXiv:2508.17438 (Ma–Zhao, 2025) resolves Babai's analogous 1979 problem for undirected vertex-transitive graphs, showing that longest cycles must intersect in at least f(n) vertices where f(n)→∞; however, that work is confined to undirected graphs and does not address the directed case. No follow-up paper resolving the directed version has been found in the indexed literature.
search_queries: vertex transitive digraph longest directed cycles intersection common vertex 2602.16333
longest cycles vertex transitive digraph intersection 2025 2026 Bucic Hendrey Mohar Steiner Yepremyan
notes: arXiv:2508.17438 (Ma–Zhao, 'Intersections of longest cycles in vertex-transitive and highly connected graphs', August 2025) is the closest related work but treats undirected graphs only; it does not cite 2602.16333 and does not address Question 4.3. The conjecture is very recent (February 2026) and no resolution was found after a broad web search.
